ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sales_orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales order */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the order was placed */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the order was placed */,
  "item_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of item sold */,
  "order_priority" TEXT /* Priority of the order (e.g., L, C, H) */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "units_sold" INTEGER /* Number of units sold */,
  "unit_price" REAL /* Price per unit */,
  "unit_cost" REAL /* Cost per unit */,
  "total_cost" REAL /* Total cost of the order */,
  "total_profit" REAL /* Total profit from the order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer who placed the order */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Address to which the order is shipped */,
  "shipping_status" TEXT /* Current status of the shipping (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Current status of the payment (e.g., pending, paid, overdue) */,
  "order_status" TEXT /* Current status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "salesperson_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the salesperson handling the order */,
  "discount" REAL /* Discount applied to the order */,
  "tax" REAL /* Tax applied to the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_orders_item_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_type_id") REFERENCES item_types ("item_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_orders_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_orders_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_orders_salesperson_id FOREIGN KEY ("salesperson_id") REFERENCES salespeople ("salesperson_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the region */,
  "region_manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager responsible for the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regions_region_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the country is located */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "country_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the country */,
  "country_manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager responsible for the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_country_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_types" (
  "item_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item type */,
  "item_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the item type */,
  "item_type_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the item type */,
  "item_type_manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager responsible for the item type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_item_types_item_type_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_type_manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., sales, finance, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "customer_email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "customer_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "customer_address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salespeople" (
  "salesperson_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salesperson */,
  "salesperson_name" TEXT /* Name of the salesperson */,
  "salesperson_email" TEXT /* Email address of the salesperson */,
  "salesperson_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the salesperson */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salesperson_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Name of the manager */,
  "manager_email" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "manager_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the manager */,
  "manager_role" TEXT /* Role of the manager (e.g., region, country, item type) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id")
)
